<skill_content name="gitlab-coding-principles">
# Skill: gitlab-coding-principles

# Load Project Principles

Evaluate ALL groups below and load principles from EVERY group that applies to
your task. Most tasks span multiple groups (e.g., a model change may need
Backend, Database, and Testing principles). DO NOT stop after the first group.
When your task involves database queries, scopes, or data access patterns,
ALWAYS load Database principles regardless of which files you are editing.

**Database:**
- **Database fundamentals (model queries, batching, sharding, partitioning, N+1)**: Read .ai/principles/distilled/database-fundamentals.md *(load for any database work)*
- **Database migration patterns and zero-downtime safety**: Read .ai/principles/distilled/database-migrations.md *(also load: .ai/principles/distilled/database-fundamentals.md)*
- **Column types, constraints, indexes, naming conventions**: Read .ai/principles/distilled/database-schema.md *(also load: .ai/principles/distilled/database-fundamentals.md)*
- **SQL performance, transactions, batching**: Read .ai/principles/distilled/database-queries.md *(also load: .ai/principles/distilled/database-fundamentals.md)*
- **ClickHouse database concerns**: Read .ai/principles/distilled/clickhouse.md *(also load: .ai/principles/distilled/database-fundamentals.md)*

**Cells:**
- **Cells architecture sharding keys, organization data ownership, routable requests, and cell isolation when adding tables or customer-owned resources**: Read .ai/principles/distilled/cells-fundamentals.md *(load for any cells work)*
- **Cells globally-unique attribute claims (Cells::Claimable), claim rollout lifecycle, and feature flags for cross-cell uniqueness**: Read .ai/principles/distilled/cells-claims.md *(also load: .ai/principles/distilled/cells-fundamentals.md)*

**Security:**
- **Security vulnerabilities and secure coding**: Read .ai/principles/distilled/security.md
- **Authentication, authorization, token handling, OAuth, SAML, identity linking, composite identity, session management, 2FA, MFA, password management**: Read .ai/principles/distilled/authentication.md

**Permissions:**
- **Where to check permissions, naming conventions, role definitions**: Read .ai/principles/distilled/permissions-fundamentals.md *(load for any permissions work)*
- **Granular PAT (GPAT) compliance for GraphQL types and mutations**: Read .ai/principles/distilled/permissions-graphql-gpat.md *(also load: .ai/principles/distilled/permissions-fundamentals.md)*
- **Granular PAT (GPAT) compliance for REST endpoints and job tokens**: Read .ai/principles/distilled/permissions-rest-gpat.md *(also load: .ai/principles/distilled/permissions-fundamentals.md)*

**Code Review:**
- **General code review practices and acceptance checklist**: Read .ai/principles/distilled/code-review.md

**Backend:**
- **Ruby/Rails style, logging, common pitfalls**: Read .ai/principles/distilled/backend-ruby.md *(load for any backend work)*
- **Service patterns, abstractions, design conventions**: Read .ai/principles/distilled/backend-architecture.md *(also load: .ai/principles/distilled/backend-ruby.md)*
- **EE/CE separation, licensing, code placement**: Read .ai/principles/distilled/backend-ee.md *(also load: .ai/principles/distilled/backend-ruby.md)*

**API:**
- **REST API design and conventions**: Read .ai/principles/distilled/rest-api.md
- **GraphQL API design and conventions**: Read .ai/principles/distilled/graphql.md

**Workers:**
- **Sidekiq worker design and reliability**: Read .ai/principles/distilled/workers.md

**CI/CD:**
- **GitLab CI/CD internals — adding pipeline configuration keywords (Ci::Config::Entry classes, feature-flag gating, JSON schema) and creating CI routing/partitioned tables. NOTE this is about GitLab's own CI implementation, not authoring a project's .gitlab-ci.yml**: Read .ai/principles/distilled/cicd-internals.md

**Frontend:**
- **Vue.js components, state management, patterns**: Read .ai/principles/distilled/frontend-vue.md *(load for any frontend work)*
- **CSS/SCSS, Tailwind, dark mode**: Read .ai/principles/distilled/frontend-style.md *(also load: .ai/principles/distilled/frontend-vue.md)*
- **HAML templates, ViewComponents, Pajamas**: Read .ai/principles/distilled/frontend-haml.md *(also load: .ai/principles/distilled/frontend-vue.md)*
- **Frontend accessibility patterns and requirements**: Read .ai/principles/distilled/frontend-a11y.md *(also load: .ai/principles/distilled/frontend-vue.md)*

**Testing:**
- **RSpec patterns, factories, shared examples**: Read .ai/principles/distilled/qa-rspec.md
- **Jest, jsdom, Vue Test Utils patterns**: Read .ai/principles/distilled/qa-jest.md

**Performance:**
- **Performance and scalability**: Read .ai/principles/distilled/performance.md

**Documentation:**
- **Documentation style and completeness**: Read .ai/principles/distilled/documentation.md

**Feature Flags:**
- **Feature flag usage and lifecycle**: Read .ai/principles/distilled/feature-flags.md

**Analytics:**
- **Analytics instrumentation and metrics**: Read .ai/principles/distilled/analytics.md

- **Code style or linting**: Read .ai/code-style.md
- **Git, commits, or branches**: Read .ai/git.md
- **CI/CD pipelines or `.gitlab-ci.yml`**: Read .ai/ci-cd.md

5: <!-- Auto-generated from docs.gitlab.com by gitlab-ai-principles-distiller — do not edit manually -->
6: 
7: > **Prerequisite:** If you haven't already, also read .ai/principles/distilled/backend-ruby.md - it contains foundational rules that apply to all backend work.
8: 
9: # Backend Architecture Principles
10: 
11: ## Checklist
12: 
13: ### Abstraction Layer Usage
14: 
15: - DO NOT use high-level Finder classes (e.g., `ProjectsFinder`) inside other Finders; use the underlying query primitives directly
16: - DO NOT call `ActiveRecord` methods (e.g., `where`, `find_by`) directly from controllers, API endpoints, service classes, finders, presenters, or serializers — only from model class/instance methods
17: - DO NOT use Presenters or Serializers inside Service classes
18: - DO NOT use Service classes, Presenters, Serializers, or Workers inside Finders
19: - DO NOT use service classes, presenters, or serializers inside model class/instance methods
20: - DO NOT invoke a Worker directly with `SomeWorker.new.perform`; use `SomeWorker.perform_async` or `SomeWorker.perform_in`
21: - DO NOT execute database queries in views; move all data retrieval into the controller or a presenter and pass the result as an instance variable
22: - DO NOT put business logic in views; extract conditionals that evaluate model state beyond `nil?`, `present?`, or boolean attribute checks into a helper, presenter, or ViewComponent
23: - Use the abstraction table to verify that each layer only calls permitted abstractions before approving cross-layer calls
24: 
25: ### Service Classes
26: 
27: - Ensure service class initializer takes the acted-upon model as the first positional argument
28: - Ensure service classes that act on behalf of a user include `current_user:` as a keyword argument
29: - Ensure the `#execute` method takes no arguments (all data passed via initializer)
30: - Ensure `#execute` returns a `ServiceResponse` object when a return value is needed
31: - Use `ServiceResponse.success` / `ServiceResponse.error` with a `message:` and optional `payload:` or `reason:`
32: - Use domain-specific `reason:` symbols in `ServiceResponse.error` (e.g., `:job_not_retriable`, `:duplicate_package`); use Rails HTTP status symbols only for common failures like `:not_found` or `:forbidden`
33: - Inherit from `BaseContainerService`, `BaseProjectService`, or `BaseGroupService` where appropriate
34: - DO NOT put business logic that changes application state in objects that are not service classes (use finders or value objects for read-only operations)
35: 
36: ### Finders
37: 
38: - Ensure finder `#execute` methods return `ActiveRecord::Relation`; add exceptions to `spec/support/finder_collection_allowlist.yml` only when necessary
39: - DO NOT reuse other finders inside a finder
40: 
41: ### Naming and Ubiquitous Language
42: 
43: - Use ubiquitous language from the product/user documentation instead of CRUD terminology when naming service classes and domain objects (e.g., `Epic::AddExistingIssueService` not `EpicIssues::CreateService`)
44: - Use CRUD names only when they match the existing ubiquitous language and are unambiguous (e.g., `Projects::CreateService`)
45: - Ensure new classes and database tables use ubiquitous language; use `self.table_name=` when model name diverges from table name
46: 
47: ### Bounded Contexts and Namespaces
48: 
49: - Ensure every Ruby class is nested inside a top-level namespace present in `config/bounded_contexts.yml`; resolve `Gitlab/BoundedContexts` RuboCop offenses by nesting into an existing context
50: - DO NOT nest feature-specific classes under `Projects::` or `Groups::` namespaces unless the concept is strictly about projects or groups themselves
51: - DO NOT use stage or group names as bounded context namespaces (feature categories can be reassigned)
52: - Define event classes and publish events within the same bounded context (top-level Ruby namespace) where the triggering feature lives
53: - Place FOSS event subscriptions in `lib/gitlab/bounded_contexts/subscriptions/[context]_subscriptions.rb`; EE-only in `ee/lib/gitlab/event_store/subscriptions/[context]_subscriptions.rb`
54: 
55: ### Omniscient Classes
56: 
57: - DO NOT add new methods or data to omniscient classes (`Project`, `User`, `MergeRequest`, `Ci::Pipeline`, or any class >1000 LOC); create a dedicated class instead
58: - Prefer thin domain wrapper objects (e.g., `AntiAbuse::UserTrustScore.new(user)`) over adding methods to large models
59: - Use dependency inversion to encapsulate related behavior in a bounded context rather than adding it to a shared model
60: 
61: ### Use-Case-Oriented Design
62: 
63: - DO NOT reuse a single service class for radically different use cases with different permissions, preconditions, or side-effects; create separate service classes per use case
64: - Ensure each service class enforces a single, cohesive set of permissions and parameters
65: 
66: ### EventStore
67: 
68: - Name events in past tense: `<DomainObject><Action>Event` (e.g., `Ci::PipelineCreatedEvent`, not `Ci::CreatePipelineEvent`); elide the domain object when obvious from the bounded context (e.g., `MergeRequest::ApprovedEvent` not `MergeRequest::MergeRequestApprovedEvent`)
69: - Define event schemas as valid JSON Schema; mark unique identifiers as `required` and all other properties as optional
70: - Publish only properties needed by subscribers; DO NOT tailor the payload to a specific subscriber
71: - Dispatch events from service classes; use model state machine transitions as an exception, not `ActiveRecord` callbacks
72: - DO NOT publish events about domain objects outside your bounded context
73: - Introduce new Sidekiq subscriber workers in a prior deployment (or behind a feature flag) before registering the subscription
74: - Use conditional dispatch (`if:` lambda) only for cheap synchronous checks; handle complex conditions inside `handle_event`
75: - Follow the multi-rollout process when renaming events, adding required properties, or removing properties (expand → migrate → contract across separate milestones)
76: - Use `publish_event` RSpec matcher to test publishers; use `it_behaves_like 'subscribes to event'` shared example to test subscribers
77: - Define CE events and publish them in CE code; define EE events and publish them in EE code; subscribers may cross CE/EE boundaries
78: - DO NOT use EventStore when logic must run synchronously as part of the main business transaction rather than as a side-effect
79: - Use `Gitlab::EventStore.publish_group` when publishing multiple events of the same type in a single transaction to reduce Sidekiq load; configure `group_size` on the subscription as needed
80: 
81: ### Modules and Instance Variables
82: 
83: - DO NOT share instance variables across multiple mixed-in modules; keep instance variables contained within the module that owns them
84: - Prefer the `@var ||= value` single-assignment pattern when memoizing in a module
85: - DO NOT use instance variables in view partials; pass locals explicitly and fetch with `local_assigns.fetch(:key)`
86: 
87: ### Abstract Methods
88: 
89: - Use `Gitlab::AbstractMethodError` (not `NotImplementedError`, `NoMethodError`, or a generic string `raise`) for abstract methods that subclasses must implement
90: - Prefer composition and duck typing over inheritance; use abstract methods only for framework integration points or shared-template components (e.g., ViewComponents)
91: 
92: ### Application Limits
93: 
94: - Add new plan limits as a column in `plan_limits` with a non-null default, then fine-tune per plan using `create_or_update_plan_limit` in a separate migration
95: - Cover all GitLab.com plans (`default`, `free`, `premium`, `premium_trial`, `ultimate`, `ultimate_trial`, `ultimate_trial_paid_customer`, `opensource`) in limit migrations; omitting a plan causes those customers to receive the default (possibly `0`/unlimited)
96: - Use `PlanLimits#exceeded?` or the `Limitable` concern to enforce limits; DO NOT implement ad-hoc count checks
97: - Use `Rack::Attack` for middleware-level rate limiting and `Gitlab::ApplicationRateLimiter` for controller/API-level throttling
98: 
99: ### Backwards Compatibility Across Updates
100: 
101: - Follow the expand-and-contract pattern for breaking changes: expand (backward-compatible addition) → migrate (update consumers) → contract (remove old code) across separate milestones
102: - DO NOT bundle expand and migrate phases into the same milestone for Sidekiq worker parameter changes (Puma may restart before Sidekiq, causing job failures)
103: - Ensure new GraphQL fields or REST API fields added in release N are not used by frontend code until release N+1 (or are behind a default-disabled feature flag, or degrade gracefully)
104: - DO NOT add a `NOT NULL` column constraint without a default value when old application nodes are still inserting rows without that column
105: - Ensure route changes follow expand-and-contract: add new route first, then generate new-format links, then remove old route
106: 
107: ### CE/EE Code Separation
108: 
109: - CE code (outside `ee/`) must not directly reference `EE::` namespaced classes
110: - EE extensions use `prepend_mod` pattern in CE files
111: - If CE code needs EE-aware behavior, use `prepend_mod` hooks or `Gitlab.ee?` guards
112: - Flag direct references to `EE::` namespaced classes in CE code (prevents FOSS build failures)
113: 
114: ### ActiveRecord Callbacks
115: 
116: - Callbacks should only modify data on the current model, not associated records
117: - Question if callback logic should be in a service layer instead
118: - Flag callbacks with side effects (external API calls, updating other records, complex business logic)
119: - Flag bulk operations on associated records in callbacks (performance concern as associations grow)
120: - Acceptable uses: data normalization on current model only (trimming whitespace, setting defaults)
121: 
122: ### Authorization
123: 
124: - Before changing authorization logic, read the existing `authorize!` / `authorize_admin!` call and verify what permission it currently enforces; the required fix may be documentation- or test-only with no code change needed
125: 
126: ## Authoritative sources
127: 
128: For the full picture, see:
129: 
130: - doc/development/reusing_abstractions.md
131: - doc/development/software_design.md
132: - doc/development/eventstore/_index.md
133: - doc/development/module_with_instance_variables.md
134: - doc/development/application_limits.md
135: - doc/development/multi_version_compatibility.md
136: 

(End of file - total 136 lines)
</content>

5: <!-- Auto-generated from docs.gitlab.com by gitlab-ai-principles-distiller — do not edit manually -->
6: 
7: # Permissions Fundamentals Principles
8: 
9: ## Checklist
10: 
11: ### Authorization Enforcement
12: 
13: - Enforce authorization using defense-in-depth: implement permission checks at multiple layers (finders, services, GraphQL, REST API, controllers).
14: - Enforce authorization in shared business logic (services and finders) as the primary layer, so checks are consistent across REST, GraphQL, and controllers.
15: - DO NOT rely solely on display-layer permission checks (such as showing/hiding buttons) as a security control — always back them with backend enforcement.
16: - Prefer domain logic (services or finders) as the source of truth when making exceptions to multi-layer enforcement.
17: - When a class accepts `current_user`, ensure it is responsible for authorization.
18: - When adding a new API endpoint, check an existing ability or add a new one; do not skip the authorization check.
19: - When using an ability check in UI elements, ensure the underlying backend code also performs the same ability check.
20: - Use `push_frontend_ability` (available on controllers inheriting from `ApplicationController`) to expose abilities to JavaScript; access them via `gon.abilities.<camelCaseName>` (DO NOT use `gon.abilities.<snake_case_name>`).
21: 
22: ### Permission Naming Conventions
23: 
24: - Introduce a new permission only when no existing permission covers the same subject and action; reuse existing permissions wherever possible.
25: - Follow the naming pattern `action_resource(_subresource)` for all permissions (both assignable and raw).
26: - Use only the preferred actions — `create`, `read`, `update`, `delete` — unless a documented exception applies; obtain Authorization team approval for any action outside this set.
27: - DO NOT introduce disallowed actions: `admin`, `change`, `configure`, `destroy`, `edit`, `list`, `manage`, `modify`, `set`, `view`, or `write`.
28: - Use the singular form for the resource segment (e.g., `read_project`, not `read_projects`).
29: - Match the resource name to the domain object being acted upon, using user-facing terminology rather than implementation details.
30: - DO NOT encode the resource boundary (`project`, `group`, `user`) into the permission name; pass the subject in the `can?` call to determine scope instead (e.g., use `read_insights_dashboard` not `read_project_insights_dashboard`).
31: - Introduce a new action outside the preferred set only when it represents a distinct lifecycle transition (`archive_project`), a relationship change (`transfer_project`), or a high-impact/irreversible operation (`purge_maven_virtual_registry_cache`) already established in GitLab domain language.
32: 
33: ### Private Permissions
34: 
35: - Prefix private permissions with an underscore (`_`) following the pattern `_<action>_<qualifier>_<resource>` (e.g., `_read_authored_issue`).
36: - DO NOT check private permissions at enforcement points (controllers, services, finders, GraphQL); use them only inside policy files.
37: - Use private permissions when a role can perform an action only when a subject-level condition is met, or when different roles have different levels of access to the same action.
38: - DO NOT use private permissions for feature flag checks, license checks, or settings-based restrictions — use `prevent` rules instead.
39: - Create a definition file for every private permission under `config/authz/permissions/<resource>/_<action>_<qualifier>.yml`.
40: - Use qualifiers that correspond to a real attribute or relationship on the resource (e.g., `confidential`, `authored`, `assigned`); DO NOT use qualifiers that describe the actor.
41: - Prefer past-participle or adjective forms for qualifiers that read naturally as a description of the resource; introduce qualifiers only when the same action on the same resource requires different access levels depending on the resource's state.
42: 
43: ### Role Definition YAML Files
44: 
45: - Define default roles as YAML files under `config/authz/roles/` with the fields `name`, `description`, `inherits_from`, and optionally `raw_permissions` and `permissions`.
46: - Understand that the final permission set for a role is the union of its `raw_permissions`, expanded `permissions` (assignable groups), and all permissions inherited recursively from parent roles.
47: - When adding a permission to a role, prefer using an assignable permission group; use `raw_permissions` only for existing permissions that need to be moved into the role definition.
48: - When removing a permission from a role, verify it is not depended on by other features; use `GITLAB_DEBUG_POLICIES=true` to trace where a permission is checked.
49: 
50: ### Custom Roles
51: 
52: - Run `./ee/bin/custom-ability <ABILITY_NAME>` to generate the YAML configuration file for a new custom ability under `ee/config/custom_abilities/`.
53: - Run `bundle exec rails generate gitlab:custom_roles:code --ability <ABILITY_NAME>` to update the permissions validation schema and create a spec file.
54: - Define permissions declaratively in the custom ability YAML using `project_permissions` and `group_permissions` fields; DO NOT manually add policy rules to `ProjectPolicy` or `GroupPolicy` for custom abilities.
55: - Mark a custom ability as `wip: true` in its YAML when implementing across multiple MRs; remove the `wip:` key when the ability is ready to ship.
56: - Consolidate custom role abilities to a minimum: use `read_*` for all view-related actions and `admin_*` for object updates; avoid introducing additional abilities unless necessary.
57: - Ensure `admin_*` custom abilities declare `read_*` as a requirement in the YAML `requirements` field.
58: - Add the ability as a trait in the `MemberRoles` factory (`ee/spec/factories/member_roles.rb`) and add request specs under `ee/spec/requests/custom_roles/<ABILITY_NAME>/request_spec.rb`.
59: - Run `bundle exec rake gitlab:custom_roles:compile_docs` and `bundle exec rake gitlab:graphql:compile_docs` to update documentation after adding a custom ability.
60: - Assess privilege escalation risk when adding new custom permissions that interact with GitLab artifacts (such as access tokens) that encode the base role.
61: 
62: ### Internal Permission Groups
63: 
64: - Define internal permission groups as YAML files under `config/authz/permission_groups/internal/<resource>/<name>.yml` with `description` and `permissions` fields.
65: - Use internal permission groups in `DeclarativePolicy` policies by calling `Authz::PermissionGroups::Internal.get('<identifier>')` and splatting the result into a `prevent` rule.
66: - Use internal permission groups when a resource state change (archiving, locking) should disable many permissions at once, or when the same permission set is referenced from multiple places.
67: - DO NOT use internal permission groups for role-based permission assignment (use role definitions or custom roles), or for token-scoped permissions (use assignable permissions).
68: 
69: ### Predefined Roles and Visibility
70: 
71: - Store project membership (with group membership already resolved) in the `project_authorizations` table; the highest permission across group and project membership is the effective access level.
72: - DO NOT grant subgroups a higher visibility level than their parent group.
73: - Design complex feature permissions as separate, granular permissions with explicit dependency relationships rather than reusing a single broad permission.
74: 
75: ### Policy Testing
76: 
77: - Place policy unit tests in `spec/policies/` (CE) or `ee/spec/policies/` (EE).
78: - Use one `describe` block per permission; DO NOT group multiple permissions into a single block.
79: - Use `where` table syntax to test each role explicitly, and include every role in the table — DO NOT omit roles expected to be disallowed.
80: - Define the subject explicitly inside each `describe` block using `let_it_be`; DO NOT rely on a subject defined at a higher scope.
81: - Use descriptively named fixtures that reflect the visibility or state under test (e.g., `private_project`, `archived_project`) rather than a generic `project`.
82: - Test both feature flag disabled and licensed feature disabled contexts for every permission that is gated by either.

5: <!-- Auto-generated from docs.gitlab.com by gitlab-ai-principles-distiller — do not edit manually -->
6: 
7: # Backend Ruby/Rails Principles
8: 
9: ## Checklist
10: 
11: ### Ruby Style
12: 
13: - Order methods by level of abstraction (high-level orchestrator methods before helper methods) within each visibility section; Exception: grouping by domain concept or alphabetical ordering for large sets of similar methods may take precedence.
14: - DO NOT nest beyond two levels of method calls in the stepdown pattern; refactor into separate classes if needed.
15: - Use `attr_reader` for public attributes only when accessed outside the class; maintain consistency for internal access.
16: - Separate code with newlines only to group related logic together; add a newline before blocks. Exception: do not add a newline when a code block starts or ends right inside another code block.
17: - Use `for_` prefix for scopes filtering by `belongs_to` associations (e.g., `scope :for_project`).
18: - Use `with_` prefix for scopes using `joins`, `includes`, or filtering by `has_one`/`has_many`/boolean conditions.
19: - Use `including_` prefix for scopes that eager-load associations via `includes` without changing the result set; use `preload_` when loading multiple `has_many` associations or when a separate subquery is explicitly required.
20: - Use `order_by_` prefix for scopes that apply `order`.
21: - Freeze constants (`CONSTANT = 'value'.freeze`).
22: - DO NOT call application logic (database queries, service calls, I18n helpers) when defining class-level constants; use a method instead so the logic runs at call time.
23: - Prefer `excluding` (alias `without`) over hand-written `where.not(id: record)` when excluding specific records already loaded in memory; DO NOT use `excluding` as a replacement for `where.not(id: relation)` — pass the relation directly to avoid loading IDs into memory.
24: 
25: ### ActiveRecord / Rails
26: 
27: - DO NOT add new lifecycle logic via ActiveRecord callbacks; put it in a service class instead. Exception: callbacks are acceptable when overriding a dependency's callback, incrementing cache counts, or normalizing data that only relates to the current model.
28: - DO NOT override `has_many through:` or `has_one through:` associations; overriding changes `destroy()` behavior and can cause data loss.
29: - DO NOT open database connections or issue queries from Rails initializers; tasks like `db:drop` and `db:test:prepare` will fail if an active session is held.
30: - DO NOT issue database queries in routes.
31: 
32: ### JSON
33: 
34: - Use `Gitlab::Json` in place of all calls to the default `JSON` class, `.to_json`, and similar methods.
35: - Use `Gitlab::Json::LimitedEncoder` when JSON output size must be bounded.
36: 
37: ### Logging
38: 
39: - DO NOT use `Rails.logger`; use a structured JSON logger instead.
40: - DO NOT use `$stdout.puts`, `$stderr.puts`, `$stdout.print`, `$stderr.print`, or equivalent `STDOUT`/`STDERR` calls in application code; use a structured JSON logger or existing wrapper methods (e.g., `SystemCheck::Helpers`) for Rake/CLI output.
41: - Use a subclass of `Gitlab::JsonLogger` for new log files; call `exclude_context!` if the logger is used outside of a request context.
42: - Pass log messages as key-value hashes, not interpolated strings (e.g., `logger.info(message: "...", project_id: id)`).
43: - Ensure field value types are consistent across all log calls for the same field key; DO NOT mix types (e.g., integer vs. string for the same field).
44: - Ensure list elements in log fields are all the same type.
45: - Include a `class` attribute in structured log payloads; use `Gitlab::Loggable` and `build_structured_payload` to add it automatically.
46: - Log durations in seconds as a float with microsecond precision; suffix the key with `_s` and include `duration` in the key name (e.g., `view_duration_s`).
47: - DO NOT manually log exceptions; use `Gitlab::ErrorTracking.track_exception` or `Gitlab::ErrorTracking.track_and_raise_exception` with additional context parameters.
48: - DO NOT increase overall log volume by more than 10% when adding new log messages.
49: - Log deprecation notices only in the development environment if the expected volume is large.
50: 
51: ### Log Field Standards
52: 
53: - Log fields should be defined within the LabKit Ruby Fields module.
54: - Common logging fields imported from `labkit-ruby` `lib/labkit/fields.rb`.
55: - New fields added to log messages must not be dynamically generated.
56: - Follow Field Standardisation Guidelines for observability.
57: 
58: ### Internationalization (i18n)
59: 
60: - Wrap all user-visible strings in Ruby/HAML with `_()`, `s_()`, or `n_()` helpers; use `__()`, `s__()`, `n__()` in JavaScript/Vue.
61: - DO NOT translate strings at class or module load time (e.g., in constants or memoized class methods); keep translations dynamic by calling helpers inside instance/class methods.
62: - DO NOT split sentences across multiple translation calls; externalize the full sentence with interpolation placeholders.
63: - DO NOT include HTML directly in translation strings; use `safe_format` with `tag_pair` in Ruby/HAML or `GlSprintf` in Vue.
64: - DO NOT call `downcase` or `toLocaleLowerCase()` on translatable strings; let translators control casing.
65: - Add a namespace (PascalCase, followed by `|`) to all UI strings to provide translator context; prefer granular subcategories over broad ones.
66: - Pass only string literals to translation helpers; DO NOT pass variables, function calls, or interpolated strings.
67: - Use `%{named}` placeholders rather than positional `%d` in strings where the number adds no value to the singular form; use `n_`/`n__` with `%{count}` named placeholders for counted strings.
68: - Use `n_`/`n__` only to select between plural forms of the same string, not to switch between entirely different strings.
69: - DO NOT place a zero-state phrase in the `one` slot of a plural string; handle the zero state as a separate string outside the plural call.
70: - Pluralize whole sentences rather than extracting single words to give translators full context.
71: - DO NOT define pluralized strings that depend on runtime counts as static constants in Vue; define them as functions that accept a `count` argument.
72: - Split strings with multiple independent plurals into separate `n__()` calls and combine with a non-pluralized connector string.
73: - Add errors to `:base` with a complete sentence rather than to a specific attribute when the message is a full sentence, to avoid Rails prepending the humanized attribute name.
74: - Update `locale/gitlab.pot` by running `tooling/bin/gettext_extractor locale/gitlab.pot` before pushing changes to translated strings.
75: - In RSpec, use the same externalizing helper in expectations (e.g., `have_content(_('...'))`); DO NOT hard-code translated strings.
76: - In Jest, DO NOT wrap expected strings in `__()` — externalization is mocked and expectations should use plain string literals.
77: 
78: ### Redis
79: 
80: - Ensure Redis keys are globally unique across all Redis categories/instances.
81: - Use immutable identifiers (e.g., project ID, not full path) in Redis key names.
82: - Enclose the changeable parts of keys in curly braces `{}` when multiple keys must reside on the same Redis shard (hash-tags for Redis Cluster compatibility).
83: - Use `Gitlab::Redis::Cache` only for truly ephemeral, regenerable data; always set a TTL explicitly (no default TTL is set; consider 8 hours to match a workday).
84: - Use `Gitlab::Redis::SharedState` for data that must persist until its expiration; always set a TTL.
85: - DO NOT use `Rails.cache` for data that must be reliably persisted; use `Gitlab::Redis::SharedState` instead.
86: - Use `RedisCommands::Recorder` in tests to detect Redis N+1 call problems and assert expected call counts.
87: 
88: ### Polling
89: 
90: - DO NOT add new features that require polling and hit the database; use ETag caching in Redis instead.
91: - Register polled endpoints in `Gitlab::EtagCaching::Router`, set the polling interval header via `Gitlab::PollingInterval.set_header`, and invalidate ETags via `Gitlab::EtagCaching::Store` on resource changes.
92: - DO NOT use query parameters on endpoints where ETag caching is enabled; include all parameters in the request path.
93: 
94: ### Routing
95: 
96: - Place every group route under the `/-/` scope.
97: - Place every project route under the `/-/` scope, except where a Git client or other external software requires otherwise.
98: - Use organization-scoped routes (`/o/:organization_path/*path`) for organization-level resources.
99: - DO NOT change an existing URL without providing a redirect; support both old and new URLs for at least one major release for script-facing URLs, and add a redirect for user-facing URLs until the next major release.
100: - Use `Gitlab::Routing.redirect_legacy_paths` when adding `/-/` scope to previously unscoped routes, and create a technical debt issue to remove deprecated routes in a later release.
101: 
102: ### Rails Initializers
103: 
104: - DO NOT open database connections or issue queries from Rails initializers; tasks like `db:drop` and `db:test:prepare` will fail if an active session is held.
105: - Place initializers that must run before Zeitwerk loads (e.g., those modifying `config.autoload_paths` or Zeitwerk inflections) in `config/initializers_before_autoloader` instead of `config/initializers`.
106: 
107: ### Code Comments
108: 
109: - Focus comments on the "why" (rationale, constraints, edge cases), not the "what" or "how".
110: - Add a link to a tracking issue in any comment describing a follow-up action or technical debt.
111: - Use YARD syntax (`@param`, `@return`) when documenting method arguments or return values.
112: - Annotate methods whose return value should not be used with `@return [void]` and explicitly `return nil` at the end.
113: 
114: ### Gotchas
115: 
116: - DO NOT read files from `app/assets` in application code; use `lib/assets` for assets that must be accessed by application code but not served directly.
117: - DO NOT assert against the absolute value of a sequence-generated factory attribute in specs; set the attribute explicitly with a value that does not match the sequence pattern.
118: - DO NOT use `expect_any_instance_of` or `allow_any_instance_of` in RSpec; use `expect_next_instance_of`, `allow_next_instance_of`, `expect_next_found_instance_of`, or `allow_next_found_instance_of` instead.
119: - DO NOT `rescue Exception`; rescue specific exception classes.
120: - DO NOT use inline JavaScript in Haml views (`:javascript` filter).
121: - DO NOT inherit from `OpenStruct`; prefer `Struct` for new code.
122: - DO NOT stub method calls on `Regexp` or `Range` instances in RSpec — they are frozen in Ruby 3 and cannot be stubbed; stub the method that returns the range/regexp instead.
123: - Pass hash entries to lambdas as a block rather than via `&method_ref` to avoid Ruby 3 argument-count errors (`Hash#each` consistently yields a 2-element array to lambdas in Ruby 3).
124: - When a method takes keyword arguments, use `f(k: v)` or `f(**{k: v})`; DO NOT use `f({k: v})` — it is only valid in Ruby 3 if `f` takes a positional `Hash`.
125: - In RSpec `with` matchers, pass an explicit `Hash` literal `{ a: 42 }` (with braces) when the method under test takes a positional options hash, not keyword arguments, to avoid Ruby 3 matcher failures.
126: 
127: ### RuboCop
128: 
129: - DO NOT disable RuboCop rules inline without providing a reason as a code comment on the same line.
130: - Use `rubocop:todo` (not `rubocop:disable`) for temporary inline disables, and link the follow-up issue or epic.
131: - When adding a new cop that could apply to multiple applications, add it to the `gitlab-styles` gem; if it only applies to the main GitLab application, add it to the GitLab repository.
132: - Include RDoc-style docs with "good" and "bad" examples when creating new internal RuboCop cops.
133: - Generate TODOs for new cops using `bundle exec rake rubocop:todo:generate` rather than adding inline disables throughout the codebase.
134: - Enable a new cop by: updating `.rubocop.yml`, generating TODOs, creating an issue to fix TODOs (with `~"quick win"` / `~"Seeking community contributions"` labels), and creating an issue to remove the grace period after 1 week of silence in `#f_rubocop`.
135: 
136: ## Authoritative sources
137: 
138: For the full picture, see:
139: 
140: - doc/development/backend/ruby_style_guide.md
141: - doc/development/gotchas.md
142: - doc/development/ruby3_gotchas.md
143: - doc/development/utilities.md
144: - doc/development/changelog.md
145: - doc/development/i18n/pluralization.md
146: - doc/development/logging.md
147: - doc/development/json.md

151: - doc/development/routing.md

251:   # Batch methods bypass callbacks for performance, so any new callbacks may need
252:   # to be handled manually.

254:   # See: https://gitlab.com/gitlab-org/gitlab/-/work_items/546431
255:   state_machine :merge_status, initial: :unchecked do
256:     event :mark_as_preparing do
257:       transition [:unchecked, :can_be_merged] => :preparing
258:     end
259: 
260:     event :mark_as_unchecked do
261:       transition [:preparing, :can_be_merged, :checking] => :unchecked
262:       transition [:cannot_be_merged, :cannot_be_merged_rechecking] => :cannot_be_merged_recheck
263:     end
264: 
265:     event :mark_as_checking do
266:       transition unchecked: :checking
267:       transition cannot_be_merged_recheck: :cannot_be_merged_rechecking
268:     end
269: 
270:     event :mark_as_mergeable do
271:       transition [:unchecked, :cannot_be_merged_recheck, :checking, :cannot_be_merged_rechecking] => :can_be_merged
272:     end

275:       transition [:unchecked, :cannot_be_merged_recheck, :checking, :cannot_be_merged_rechecking] => :cannot_be_merged
276:     end

278:     state :preparing
279:     state :unchecked
280:     state :cannot_be_merged_recheck

330:   # NOTE: Batch transition merge_status to unchecked/cannot_be_merged_recheck.
331:   # This bypasses state machine callbacks for performance but mimics their behavior.
332:   #
333:   # Transitions mirror the `mark_as_unchecked` event defined above.
334:   # Process batch updates in batches as there can be many target merge requests.
335:   def self.batch_mark_as_unchecked(mr_ids, mrs_to_trigger: [], batch_size: 1000)
336:     return if mr_ids.blank?
337: 
338:     mr_ids.each_slice(batch_size) do |batch_ids|
339:       where(id: batch_ids)
340:         .where(merge_status: [:preparing, :can_be_merged, :checking])
341:         .update_all(merge_status: :unchecked)
342: 
343:       where(id: batch_ids)

346:     end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module MergeRequests
4:   class CreateService < MergeRequests::BaseService
5:     def execute
6:       set_projects!
7:       set_default_attributes!
8: 
9:       merge_request = MergeRequest.new
10:       merge_request.target_project = @project
11:       merge_request.source_project = @source_project
12:       merge_request.source_branch = params[:source_branch]
13: 
14:       merge_after = params.delete(:merge_after)
15: 
16:       created_merge_request = create(merge_request)
17: 
18:       UpdateMergeScheduleService.new(created_merge_request, merge_after: merge_after).execute
19: 
20:       created_merge_request
21:     end
22: 
23:     def after_create(issuable)
24:       invalidate_all_users_cache_count(issuable)
25: 
26:       current_user_id = current_user.id
27: 
28:       issuable.run_after_commit do
29:         # Add new items to MergeRequests::AfterCreateService if they can
30:         # be performed in Sidekiq
31:         NewMergeRequestWorker.perform_async(issuable.id, current_user_id)
32:       end
33: 
34:       issuable.mark_as_preparing
35: 
36:       super
37:     end
38: 
39:     # expose issuable create method so it can be called from email
40:     # handler CreateMergeRequestHandler
41:     public :create
42: 
43:     private
44: 
45:     def before_create(merge_request)
46:       # If the fetching of the source branch occurs in an ActiveRecord
47:       # callback (e.g. after_create), a database transaction will be
48:       # open while the Gitaly RPC waits. To avoid an idle in transaction
49:       # timeout, we do this before we attempt to save the merge request.
50: 
51:       merge_request.source_branch_exists?
52:       merge_request.target_branch_exists?
53: 
54:       merge_request.skip_ensure_merge_request_diff = true
55:       merge_request.check_for_spam(user: current_user, action: :create)
56:     end
57: 
58:     def set_projects!
59:       # @project is used to determine whether the user can set the merge request's
60:       # assignee, milestone and labels. Whether they can depends on their
61:       # permissions on the target project.
62:       @source_project = @project
63:       @project = Project.find(params[:target_project_id]) if params[:target_project_id]
64: 
65:       # make sure that source/target project ids are not in
66:       # params so it can't be overridden later when updating attributes
67:       # from params when applying quick actions
68:       params.delete(:source_project_id)
69:       params.delete(:target_project_id)
70: 
71:       unless can?(current_user, :create_merge_request_from, @source_project) &&
72:           can?(current_user, :create_merge_request_in, @project)
73: 
74:         raise Gitlab::Access::AccessDeniedError
75:       end
76:     end
77: 
78:     def set_default_attributes!
79:       set_default_squash! unless params.key?(:squash) || params.key?('squash')
80:       set_default_force_remove_source_branch! if params[:force_remove_source_branch].nil?
81:     end
82: 
83:     def set_default_force_remove_source_branch!
84:       params[:force_remove_source_branch] = @source_project.remove_source_branch_after_merge?
85:     end
86: 
87:     def set_default_squash!
88:       params[:squash] = @project.squash_enabled_by_default?
89:     end
90:   end
91: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module MergeRequests
4:   class AfterCreateService < MergeRequests::BaseService
5:     def execute(merge_request)
6:       merge_request.ensure_merge_request_diff
7: 
8:       prepare_for_mergeability(merge_request)
9:       prepare_merge_request(merge_request)
10:       mark_merge_request_as_prepared(merge_request)
11: 
12:       logger.info(**log_payload(merge_request, 'Executing hooks'))
13:       execute_hooks(merge_request)
14:       logger.info(**log_payload(merge_request, 'Executed hooks'))
15:     end
16: 
17:     private
18: 
19:     def prepare_for_mergeability(merge_request)
20:       if async_pipeline_creation?(merge_request)
21:         logger.info(**log_payload(merge_request, 'Creating pipeline async'))
22:         create_pipeline_for(merge_request, current_user, async: true)
23:         logger.info(**log_payload(merge_request, 'Pipeline creating async'))
24:       else
25:         logger.info(**log_payload(merge_request, 'Creating pipeline'))
26:         create_pipeline_for(merge_request, current_user, async: false)
27:         merge_request.update_head_pipeline
28:         logger.info(**log_payload(merge_request, 'Pipeline created'))
29:       end
30: 
31:       check_mergeability(merge_request)
32:     end
33: 
34:     def prepare_merge_request(merge_request)
35:       event_service.open_mr(merge_request, current_user)
36: 
37:       merge_request_activity_counter.track_create_mr_action(user: current_user, merge_request: merge_request)
38:       merge_request_activity_counter.track_mr_including_ci_config(user: current_user, merge_request: merge_request)
39: 
40:       notification_service.new_merge_request(merge_request, current_user)
41:       merge_request.diffs(include_stats: false).write_cache
42:       merge_request.create_cross_references!(current_user)
43:       todo_service.new_merge_request(merge_request, current_user)
44:       merge_request.cache_merge_request_closes_issues!(current_user)
45: 
46:       Gitlab::InternalEvents.track_event(
47:         'create_merge_request',
48:         user: current_user,
49:         project: merge_request.target_project
50:       )
51: 
52:       link_lfs_objects(merge_request)
53:     end
54: 
55:     def link_lfs_objects(merge_request)
56:       LinkLfsObjectsService.new(project: merge_request.target_project).execute(merge_request)
57:     end
58: 
59:     def check_mergeability(merge_request)
60:       return unless merge_request.preparing?
61: 
62:       # Need to set to unchecked to be able to check for mergeability or else
63:       # it'll be a no-op.
64:       merge_request.mark_as_unchecked
65:       merge_request.check_mergeability(async: true)
66:     end
67: 
68:     def mark_merge_request_as_prepared(merge_request)
69:       merge_request.update!(prepared_at: Time.current)
70:       GraphqlTriggers.merge_request_merge_status_updated(merge_request)
71:     end
72: 
73:     def logger
74:       @logger ||= Gitlab::AppLogger
75:     end
76: 
77:     def log_payload(merge_request, message)
78:       Gitlab::ApplicationContext.current.merge(
79:         merge_request_id: merge_request.id,
80:         message: message
81:       )
82:     end
83: 
84:     def async_pipeline_creation?(merge_request)
85:       Feature.enabled?(:async_mr_pipeline_creation, merge_request.target_project)
86:     end
87:   end
88: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module MergeRequests
4:   class UpdateService < MergeRequests::BaseService
5:     include Gitlab::InternalEventsTracking
6:     extend ::Gitlab::Utils::Override
7: 
8:     def initialize(project:, current_user: nil, params: {})
9:       super
10: 
11:       @target_branch_was_deleted = @params.delete(:target_branch_was_deleted)
12:     end
13: 
14:     def execute(merge_request)
15:       if Gitlab::Utils.to_boolean(params[:draft])
16:         merge_request.title = merge_request.draft_title
17:       end
18: 
19:       if params.key?(:merge_after)
20:         merge_after = params.delete(:merge_after)
21:         UpdateMergeScheduleService.new(merge_request, merge_after: merge_after).execute
22:       end
23: 
24:       update_merge_request_with_specialized_service(merge_request) || general_fallback(merge_request)
25:     end
26: 
27:     def handle_changes(merge_request, options)
28:       super
29:       old_associations = options.fetch(:old_associations, {})
30:       old_labels = old_associations.fetch(:labels, [])
31:       old_mentioned_users = old_associations.fetch(:mentioned_users, [])
32:       old_assignees = old_associations.fetch(:assignees, [])
33:       old_reviewers = old_associations.fetch(:reviewers, [])
34:       old_timelogs = old_associations.fetch(:timelogs, [])
35:       changed_fields = merge_request.previous_changes.keys
36: 
37:       resolve_todos(merge_request, old_labels, old_assignees, old_reviewers)
38: 
39:       if merge_request.previous_changes.include?('title') ||
40:           merge_request.previous_changes.include?('description')
41:         todo_service.update_merge_request(merge_request, current_user, old_mentioned_users)
42:         handle_title_and_desc_edits(merge_request, merge_request.previous_changes.keys)
43:       end
44: 
45:       handle_target_branch_change(merge_request, changed_fields)
46:       handle_draft_status_change(merge_request, changed_fields)
47: 
48:       track_title_and_desc_edits(changed_fields)
49:       track_discussion_lock_toggle(merge_request, changed_fields)
50:       track_time_estimate_and_spend_edits(merge_request, old_timelogs, changed_fields)
51:       track_labels_change(merge_request, old_labels)
52: 
53:       notify_if_labels_added(merge_request, old_labels)
54:       notify_if_mentions_added(merge_request, old_mentioned_users)
55: 
56:       # Since #mark_as_unchecked triggers an update action through the MR's
57:       #   state machine, we want to push this as far down in the process so we
58:       #   avoid resetting #ActiveModel::Dirty
59:       #
60:       if changed_fields.include?('target_branch') ||
61:           changed_fields.include?('source_branch')
62:         merge_request.mark_as_unchecked unless merge_request.unchecked?
63: 
64:         # Delete HEAD diff and re-enqueue mergeability check so we get to generate
65:         # a fresh HEAD diff when either branches have been changed.
66:         merge_request.merge_head_diff&.destroy!
67:         merge_request.check_mergeability(async: true)
68:       end
69:     end
70: 
71:     def handle_task_changes(merge_request)
72:       todo_service.resolve_todos_for_target(merge_request, current_user)
73:       todo_service.update_merge_request(merge_request, current_user)
74:     end
75: 
76:     def after_update(merge_request, old_associations)
77:       super
78: 
79:       merge_request.cache_merge_request_closes_issues!(current_user) unless merge_request.auto_merge_enabled?
80:       @trigger_work_item_updated = true
81:     end
82: 
83:     private
84: 
85:     attr_reader :target_branch_was_deleted
86: 
87:     def trigger_updated_work_item_on_closing_issues(merge_request, old_closing_issues_ids)
88:       new_issue_ids = merge_request.merge_request_closing_issues.limit(1000).pluck(:issue_id) # rubocop:disable CodeReuse/ActiveRecord -- Implementation would be the same in the model
89:       all_issue_ids = new_issue_ids | old_closing_issues_ids
90:       return if all_issue_ids.blank?
91: 
92:       WorkItem.id_in(all_issue_ids).find_each(batch_size: 100) do |work_item| # rubocop:disable CodeReuse/ActiveRecord -- Implementation would be the same in the model
93:         GraphqlTriggers.work_item_updated(work_item)
94:       end
95:     end
96: 
97:     override :associations_before_update
98:     def associations_before_update(merge_request)
99:       super.merge(
100:         closing_issues_ids: merge_request.merge_request_closing_issues.limit(1000).pluck(:issue_id) # rubocop:disable CodeReuse/ActiveRecord -- Implementation would be the same in the model
101:       )
102:     end
103: 
104:     override :change_state
105:     def change_state(merge_request, state_event)
106:       return unless super
107: 
108:       @trigger_work_item_updated = true
109:     end
110: 
111:     override :trigger_update_subscriptions
112:     def trigger_update_subscriptions(merge_request, old_associations)
113:       return unless @trigger_work_item_updated
114: 
115:       trigger_updated_work_item_on_closing_issues(
116:         merge_request,
117:         old_associations.fetch(:closing_issues_ids, [])
118:       )
119:     end
120: 
121:     def general_fallback(merge_request)
122:       # We don't allow change of source/target projects and source branch
123:       # after merge request was created
124:       params.delete(:source_project_id)
125:       params.delete(:target_project_id)
126:       params.delete(:source_branch)
127: 
128:       if merge_request.closed_or_merged_without_fork?
129:         params.delete(:target_branch)
130:         params.delete(:force_remove_source_branch)
131:       end
132: 
133:       update_task_event(merge_request) || update(merge_request)
134:     end
135: 
136:     def handle_title_and_desc_edits(merge_request, changed_fields)
137:       fields = %w[title description]
138: 
139:       return unless changed_fields.any? { |field| fields.include?(field) }
140:       return unless merge_request.auto_merge_enabled?
141:       return unless should_publish_update_event?(merge_request, changed_fields)
142: 
143:       ::Gitlab::EventStore.publish(
144:         ::MergeRequests::AutoMerge::TitleDescriptionUpdateEvent.new(data: { current_user_id: current_user.id, merge_request_id: merge_request.id })
145:       )
146:     end
147: 
148:     def should_publish_update_event?(merge_request, changed_fields)
149:       changed_fields.include?('title') &&
150:         merge_request.project.merge_request_title_regex.present?
151:     end
152: 
153:     def track_title_and_desc_edits(changed_fields)
154:       tracked_fields = %w[title description]
155: 
156:       return unless changed_fields.any? { |field| tracked_fields.include?(field) }
157: 
158:       tracked_fields.each do |action|
159:         next unless changed_fields.include?(action)
160: 
161:         merge_request_activity_counter
162:           .public_send(:"track_#{action}_edit_action", user: current_user) # rubocop:disable GitlabSecurity/PublicSend
163:       end
164:     end
165: 
166:     def track_discussion_lock_toggle(merge_request, changed_fields)
167:       return unless changed_fields.include?('discussion_locked')
168: 
169:       if merge_request.discussion_locked
170:         merge_request_activity_counter.track_discussion_locked_action(user: current_user)
171:       else
172:         merge_request_activity_counter.track_discussion_unlocked_action(user: current_user)
173:       end
174:     end
175: 
176:     def track_time_estimate_and_spend_edits(merge_request, old_timelogs, changed_fields)
177:       merge_request_activity_counter.track_time_estimate_changed_action(user: current_user) if changed_fields.include?('time_estimate')
178:       merge_request_activity_counter.track_time_spent_changed_action(user: current_user) if old_timelogs != merge_request.timelogs
179:     end
180: 
181:     def track_labels_change(merge_request, old_labels)
182:       return if Set.new(merge_request.labels) == Set.new(old_labels)
183: 
184:       merge_request_activity_counter.track_labels_changed_action(user: current_user)
185:     end
186: 
187:     def notify_if_labels_added(merge_request, old_labels)
188:       added_labels = merge_request.labels - old_labels
189: 
190:       return unless added_labels.present?
191: 
192:       notification_service.async.relabeled_merge_request(
193:         merge_request,
194:         added_labels,
195:         current_user
196:       )
197:     end
198: 
199:     def notify_if_mentions_added(merge_request, old_mentioned_users)
200:       added_mentions = merge_request.mentioned_users(current_user) - old_mentioned_users

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class MergeRequestPolicy < IssuablePolicy
4:   condition(:can_approve) { can_approve? }
5: 
6:   rule { locked }.policy do
7:     prevent :reopen_merge_request
8:   end
9: 
10:   # Only users who can read the merge request can comment.
11:   # Although :read_merge_request is computed in the policy context,
12:   # it would not be safe to prevent :create_note there, since
13:   # note permissions are shared, and this would apply too broadly.
14:   rule { ~can?(:read_merge_request) }.policy do
15:     prevent :create_note
16:     prevent :accept_merge_request
17:     prevent :mark_note_as_internal
18:   end
19: 
20:   rule { can_approve }.policy do
21:     enable :approve_merge_request
22:   end
23: 
24:   rule { can?(:approve_merge_request) & bot }.policy do
25:     enable :reset_merge_request_approvals
26:   end
27: 
28:   rule { ~anonymous & can?(:read_merge_request) }.policy do
29:     enable :create_todo
30:     enable :update_subscription
31:   end
32: 
33:   rule { hidden & ~admin }.policy do
34:     prevent :read_merge_request
35:     prevent :update_merge_request
36:     prevent :approve_merge_request
37:   end
38: 
39:   condition(:can_merge) { @subject.can_be_merged_by?(@user) }
40: 
41:   rule { can_merge }.policy do
42:     enable :accept_merge_request
43:   end
44: 
45:   rule { can?(:admin_merge_request) }.policy do
46:     enable :set_merge_request_metadata
47:   end
48: 
49:   private
50: 
51:   def can_approve?
52:     can?(:update_merge_request) && is_container_member?
53:   end
54: end
55: 
56: MergeRequestPolicy.prepend_mod_with('MergeRequestPolicy')

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class IssuablePolicy < BasePolicy
4:   delegate { subject_container }
5: 
6:   condition(:locked, scope: :subject, score: 0) { @subject.discussion_locked? }
7:   condition(:is_container_member) { subject_container.member?(@user) }
8:   condition(:can_read_issuable) { can?(:"read_#{@subject.to_ability_name}") }
9: 
10:   desc "User is the assignee or author"
11:   condition(:assignee_or_author) do
12:     @user && @subject.assignee_or_author?(@user)
13:   end
14: 
15:   condition(:is_author) { @subject.author == @user }
16:   condition(:is_assignee) { @user && @subject.assignee?(@user) }
17: 
18:   condition(:is_incident) { @subject.incident_type_issue? }
19: 
20:   desc "Issuable is hidden"
21:   condition(:hidden, scope: :subject) { @subject.hidden? }
22: 
23:   rule { is_incident }.policy do
24:     prevent :_read_assigned_work_item
25:     prevent :_read_authored_work_item
26: 
27:     prevent :_reopen_assigned_work_item
28:     prevent :_reopen_authored_work_item
29: 
30:     prevent :_update_assigned_work_item
31:     prevent :_update_authored_work_item
32:   end
33: 
34:   rule { can?(:_read_assigned_work_item) & is_assignee }.enable :read_issue
35:   rule { can?(:_read_authored_work_item) & is_author }.enable :read_issue
36: 
37:   rule { can?(:_update_assigned_work_item) & is_assignee }.enable :update_issue
38:   rule { can?(:_update_authored_work_item) & is_author }.enable :update_issue
39: 
40:   rule { can?(:_reopen_assigned_work_item) & is_assignee }.enable :reopen_issue
41:   rule { can?(:_reopen_authored_work_item) & is_author }.enable :reopen_issue
42: 
43:   rule { can?(:read_merge_request) & assignee_or_author }.policy do
44:     enable :update_merge_request
45:     enable :reopen_merge_request
46:   end
47: 
48:   rule { locked & ~is_container_member }.policy do
49:     prevent :create_note
50:     prevent :admin_note
51:     prevent :award_emoji
52:   end
53: 
54:   rule { can?(:read_issue) }.policy do
55:     enable :read_incident_management_timeline_event
56:   end
57: 
58:   rule { ~can_read_issuable }.policy do
59:     prevent :create_timelog
60:     prevent :admin_incident_management_timeline_event
61:   end
62: 
63:   rule { can_read_issuable }.policy do
64:     enable :read_issuable
65:     enable :read_issuable_participables
66:   end
67: 
68:   def subject_container
69:     @subject.project || @subject.try(:namespace)
70:   end
71: end
72: 
73: IssuablePolicy.prepend_mod_with('IssuablePolicy')

3: # IssuableFinder
4: #
5: # Used to filter Issues and MergeRequests collections by set of params
6: #
7: # Note: This class is NOT meant to be instantiated. Instead you should
8: #       look at IssuesFinder or EpicsFinder, which inherit from this.
9: #
10: # Arguments:
11: #   klass - actual class like Issue or MergeRequest
12: #   current_user - which user use
13: #   params:
14: #     scope: 'created_by_me' or 'assigned_to_me' or 'all'
15: #     state: 'opened' or 'closed' or 'locked' or 'all'
16: #     group_id: integer
17: #     project_id: integer
18: #     milestone_title: string (cannot be simultaneously used with milestone_wildcard_id)
19: #     milestone_wildcard_id: 'none', 'any', 'upcoming', 'started' (cannot be simultaneously used with milestone_title)
20: #     release_tag: string
21: #     author_id: integer
22: #     author_username: username string or a group handle (e.g., '@group/subgroup')
23: #     assignee_id: integer or 'None' or 'Any'
24: #     closed_by_id: integer
25: #     assignee_username: string
26: #     search: string
27: #     in: 'title', 'description', or a string joining them with comma
28: #     label_name: string
29: #     sort: string
30: #     non_archived: boolean
31: #     iids: integer[]
32: #     my_reaction_emoji: string
33: #     created_after: datetime
34: #     created_before: datetime
35: #     updated_after: datetime
36: #     updated_before: datetime
37: #     attempt_group_search_optimizations: boolean
38: #     attempt_project_search_optimizations: boolean
39: #     crm_contact_id: integer
40: #     crm_organization_id: integer
41: #
42: class IssuableFinder
43:   prepend FinderWithCrossProjectAccess
44:   include FinderMethods
45:   include CreatedAtFilter
46:   include Gitlab::Utils::StrongMemoize
47:   include UpdatedAtFilter
48: 
49:   requires_cross_project_access unless: -> { params.project? }
50: 
51:   FULL_TEXT_SEARCH_TERM_PATTERN = '[\u0000-\u02FF\u1E00-\u1EFF\u2070-\u218F]*'
52:   FULL_TEXT_SEARCH_TERM_REGEX = /\A#{FULL_TEXT_SEARCH_TERM_PATTERN}\z/
53:   NEGATABLE_PARAMS_HELPER_KEYS = %i[project_id scope status include_subgroups].freeze
54: 
55:   attr_accessor :current_user, :params
56:   attr_reader :original_params
57:   attr_writer :parent
58: 
59:   delegate(*%i[milestones], to: :params)
60: 
61:   class << self
62:     def scalar_params
63:       @scalar_params ||= %i[
64:         assignee_id
65:         closed_by_id
66:         assignee_username
67:         author_id
68:         author_username
69:         crm_contact_id

72:         label_name
73:         milestone_title
74:         release_tag
75:         my_reaction_emoji
76:         search
77:         subscribed
78:       ]
79:     end

3: module MergeRequests
4:   class BaseService < ::IssuableBaseService
5:     extend ::Gitlab::Utils::Override
6:     include MergeRequests::AssignsMergeParams
7:     include MergeRequests::ErrorLogger
8:     include Gitlab::Utils::StrongMemoize
9: 
10:     delegate :repository, to: :project
11: 
12:     def initialize(project:, current_user: nil, params: {})
13:       super(container: project, current_user: current_user, params: params)
14:     end
15: 
16:     def create_note(merge_request, state = merge_request.state)
17:       SystemNoteService.change_status(merge_request, merge_request.target_project, current_user, state, nil)
18:     end
19: 
20:     def hook_data(merge_request, action, old_rev: nil, old_associations: {}, system: false, system_action: nil)
21:       # NOTE: actioned_at represents when the webhook action occurred.
22:       #   Setting it to Time.current to capture when the webhook was fired for now, but we can consider allowing each service
23:       #   to provide relevant timestamps to override this later if more precision is required (e.g., approved_at, merged_at, closed_at).
24:       actioned_at = Time.current
25: 
26:       hook_data = merge_request.to_hook_data(current_user, old_associations: old_associations, action: action, actioned_at: actioned_at)
27: 
28:       if old_rev && !Gitlab::Git.blank_ref?(old_rev)
29:         hook_data[:object_attributes][:oldrev] = old_rev
30:       end
31: 
32:       hook_data[:object_attributes][:system] = system
33:       hook_data[:object_attributes][:system_action] = system_action if system && system_action
34: 
35:       hook_data
36:     end
37: 
38:     def execute_hooks(merge_request, action = 'open', old_rev: nil, old_associations: {}, system: false, system_action: nil)
39:       # NOTE: Due to the async merge request diffs generation, we need to skip this for CreateService and execute it in
40:       #   AfterCreateService instead so that the webhook consumers receive the update when diffs are ready.
41:       return if merge_request.skip_ensure_merge_request_diff
42: 
43:       merge_data = Gitlab::Lazy.new { hook_data(merge_request, action, old_rev: old_rev, old_associations: old_associations, system: system, system_action: system_action) }
44:       merge_request.project.execute_hooks(merge_data, :merge_request_hooks)
45:       merge_request.project.execute_integrations(merge_data, :merge_request_hooks)
46: 
47:       execute_external_hooks(merge_request, merge_data)
48:       execute_group_mention_hooks(merge_request, merge_data) if action == 'open'
49: 
50:       enqueue_jira_connect_messages_for(merge_request)
51:     end
52: 
53:     def execute_external_hooks(merge_request, merge_data)
54:       # Implemented in EE
55:     end
56: 
57:     def execute_group_mention_hooks(merge_request, merge_data)
58:       return unless merge_request.instance_of?(MergeRequest)
59: 
60:       args = {
61:         'mentionable_type' => 'MergeRequest',
62:         'mentionable_id' => merge_request.id,
63:         'hook_data' => merge_data,
64:         'is_confidential' => false
65:       }
66: 
67:       merge_request.run_after_commit_or_now do
68:         Integrations::GroupMentionWorker.perform_async(args)
69:       end
70:     end
71: 
72:     def handle_changes(merge_request, options)
73:       old_associations = options.fetch(:old_associations, {})
74:       old_assignees = old_associations.fetch(:assignees, [])
75:       old_reviewers = old_associations.fetch(:reviewers, [])
76: 
77:       handle_assignees_change(merge_request, old_assignees) if merge_request.assignees != old_assignees
78:       handle_reviewers_change(merge_request, old_reviewers) if merge_request.reviewers != old_reviewers
79:     end
80: 
81:     def handle_assignees_change(merge_request, old_assignees)
82:       MergeRequests::HandleAssigneesChangeService
83:         .new(project: project, current_user: current_user)
84:         .async_execute(merge_request, old_assignees)
85:     end
86: 
87:     def handle_reviewers_change(merge_request, old_reviewers)
88:       affected_reviewers = (old_reviewers + merge_request.reviewers) - (old_reviewers & merge_request.reviewers)
89:       create_reviewer_note(merge_request, old_reviewers)
90:       notification_service.async.changed_reviewer_of_merge_request(merge_request, current_user, old_reviewers)
91:       todo_service.reassigned_reviewable(merge_request, current_user, old_reviewers)
92:       invalidate_cache_counts(merge_request, users: affected_reviewers.compact)
93:       invalidate_cache_counts(merge_request, users: merge_request.assignees)
94: 
95:       new_reviewers = merge_request.reviewers - old_reviewers
96:       merge_request_activity_counter.track_users_review_requested(users: new_reviewers)
97:       merge_request_activity_counter.track_reviewers_changed_action(user: current_user)
98:       trigger_merge_request_reviewers_updated(merge_request)
99: 
100:       set_reviewers_approved(merge_request, new_reviewers) if new_reviewers.any?
101:       set_first_reviewer_assigned_at_metrics(merge_request) if new_reviewers.any?
102:       trigger_user_merge_request_updated(merge_request)
103:       publish_assigned_reviewers_event(merge_request, new_reviewers) if new_reviewers.any?
104:     end
105: 
106:     def cleanup_environments(merge_request)
107:       Environments::StopService.new(merge_request.source_project, current_user)
108:                                .execute_for_merge_request_pipeline(merge_request)
109:     end
110: 
111:     def cancel_review_app_jobs!(merge_request)
112:       environments = merge_request.environments_in_head_pipeline.in_review_folder.available
113:       environments.each { |environment| environment.cancel_deployment_jobs! }
114:     end
115: 
116:     def source_project
117:       @source_project ||= merge_request.source_project
118:     end
119: 
120:     def target_project
121:       @target_project ||= merge_request.target_project
122:     end
123: 
124:     # Don't try to print expensive instance variables.
125:     def inspect
126:       return "#<#{self.class}>" unless respond_to?(:merge_request) && merge_request
127: 
128:       "#<#{self.class} #{merge_request.to_reference(full: true)}>"
129:     end
130: 
131:     def merge_request_activity_counter
132:       Gitlab::UsageDataCounters::MergeRequestActivityUniqueCounter
133:     end
134: 
135:     def deactivate_pages_deployments(merge_request)
136:       Pages::DeactivateMrDeploymentsWorker.perform_async(merge_request.id)
137:     end
138: 
139:     private
140: 
141:     def self.constructor_container_arg(value)
142:       { project: value }
143:     end
144: 
145:     def refresh_pipelines_on_merge_requests(merge_request, allow_duplicate: false)
146:       create_pipeline_for(merge_request, current_user, async: true, allow_duplicate: allow_duplicate)
147:     end
148: 
149:     def enqueue_jira_connect_messages_for(merge_request)
150:       return unless project.jira_subscription_exists?
151: 
152:       if Atlassian::JiraIssueKeyExtractor.has_keys?(merge_request.title, merge_request.description)
153:         JiraConnect::SyncMergeRequestWorker.perform_async(merge_request.id, Atlassian::JiraConnect::Client.generate_update_sequence_id)
154:       end
155:     end
156: 
157:     def create(merge_request)
158:       self.params = assign_allowed_merge_params(merge_request, params)
159: 
160:       super
161:     end
162: 
163:     def set_issuable_author(issuable)
164:       params[:author] ||= Gitlab::Auth::Identity.resolve_composite_identity_actor(current_user)
165: 
166:       super
167:     end
168: 
169:     def update(merge_request)
170:       self.params = assign_allowed_merge_params(merge_request, params)
171: 
172:       super
173:     end
174: 
175:     override :handle_quick_actions
176:     def handle_quick_actions(merge_request)
177:       super
178:       handle_draft_event(merge_request)
179:     end
180: 
181:     def handle_draft_event(merge_request)
182:       if draft_event = params.delete(:wip_event)
183:         # We update the title that is provided in the params or we use the mr title
184:         title = params[:title] || merge_request.title
185:         params[:title] = case draft_event
186:                          when 'draft' then MergeRequest.draft_title(title)
187:                          when 'ready' then MergeRequest.draftless_title(title)
188:                          end
189:       end
190:     end
191: 
192:     def request_duo_code_review(merge_request)
193:       # Overridden in EE
194:     end
195: 
196:     def filter_params(merge_request)
197:       super
198: 
199:       unless merge_request.can_allow_collaboration?(current_user)
200:         params.delete(:allow_collaboration)
201:       end
202: 
203:       filter_reviewer(merge_request)
204:     end
205: 
206:     def filter_reviewer(merge_request)
207:       return if params[:reviewer_ids].blank?
208: 
209:       unless can_admin_issuable?(merge_request)
210:         params.delete(:reviewer_ids)
211: 
212:         return
213:       end
214: 
215:       unless merge_request.allows_multiple_reviewers?
216:         params[:reviewer_ids] = params[:reviewer_ids].first(1)
217:       end
218: 
219:       reviewer_ids = User.id_in(params[:reviewer_ids]).select do |reviewer|
220:         link_composite_identity(reviewer) if reviewer.composite_identity_enforced? && reviewer.service_account?
221: 
222:         user_can_read?(merge_request, reviewer)
223:       end.map(&:id)
224: 
225:       if params[:reviewer_ids].map(&:to_s) == [IssuableFinder::Params::NONE]
226:         params[:reviewer_ids] = []
227:       elsif reviewer_ids.any?
228:         params[:reviewer_ids] = reviewer_ids
229:       else
230:         params.delete(:reviewer_ids)
231:       end
232:     end
233: 
234:     def set_reviewers_approved(merge_request, new_reviewers)
235:       approval_users = merge_request.approvals_for_user_ids(new_reviewers.map(&:id))
236: 
237:       merge_request.merge_request_reviewers_with(approval_users.select(:user_id))
238:         .update_all(state: :approved)
239:     end

243:     end

246:       SystemNoteService.change_issuable_assignees(

248:     end

252:         merge_request, merge_request.project, current_user, old_reviewers)
253:     end

255:     def create_pipeline_for(merge_request, user, async: false, allow_duplicate: false)
256:       create_pipeline_params = params.slice(:push_options, :gitaly_context).merge(allow_duplicate: allow_duplicate)
257:       service = MergeRequests::CreatePipelineService.new(
258:         project: project, current_user: user, params: create_pipeline_params
259:       )
260: 
261:       if async
262:         service.execute_async(merge_request)
263:       else
264:         service.execute(merge_request)
265:       end
266:     end
267: 
268:     def abort_auto_merge(merge_request, reason)
269:       AutoMergeService.new(project, current_user).abort(merge_request, reason)
270:     end
271: 
272:     # Returns all origin and fork merge requests from `@project` satisfying passed arguments.
273:     # rubocop: disable CodeReuse/ActiveRecord
274:     def merge_requests_for(source_branch, mr_states: [:opened])
275:       @project.source_of_merge_requests
276:         .with_state(mr_states)
277:         .where(source_branch: source_branch)
278:         .preload(:source_project) # we don't need #includes since we're just preloading for the #select
279:         .select(&:source_project)
280:     end
281:     # rubocop: enable CodeReuse/ActiveRecord
282: 
283:     def pipeline_merge_requests(pipeline)
284:       pipeline.all_merge_requests.opened.each do |merge_request|
285:         next unless pipeline.id == merge_request.head_pipeline_id
286: 
287:         yield merge_request
288:       end
289:     end
290: 
291:     def trigger_merge_request_reviewers_updated(merge_request)
292:       GraphqlTriggers.merge_request_reviewers_updated(merge_request)
293:     end
294: 
295:     def trigger_merge_request_merge_status_updated(merge_request)
296:       GraphqlTriggers.merge_request_merge_status_updated(merge_request)
297:     end

304:       [merge_request.author, *merge_request.assignees, *merge_request.reviewers].uniq.each do |user|
305:         GraphqlTriggers.user_merge_request_updated(user, merge_request)
306:       end

310:       # No consumers for this event yet. See here for more information
311:       # See here: https://gitlab.com/gitlab-org/gitlab/-/work_items/599509
312: 
313:       assigned_reviewers_event = AssignedReviewersEvent.build(
314:         current_user: current_user,
315:         merge_request: merge_request,
316:         new_reviewers: new_reviewers

322:       metrics = merge_request.metrics

326: 
327:       return if metrics.reviewer_first_assigned_at && metrics.reviewer_first_assigned_at <= current_time

329:       metrics.update(reviewer_first_assigned_at: current_time)

333:       MergeRequests::RemoveApprovalService.new(project: project, current_user: user)

# frozen_string_literal: true

class Projects::MergeRequests::ApplicationController < Projects::ApplicationController
  before_action :check_merge_requests_available!
  before_action :merge_request
  before_action :authorize_read_merge_request!

  feature_category :code_review_workflow

  helper_method :rapid_diffs_presenter

  before_action do
    push_force_frontend_feature_flag(:glql_load_on_click, !!project&.glql_load_on_click_feature_flag_enabled?)
  end

  private

  PIPELINE_DISPLAY_LIMIT = 100

  def rapid_diffs_presenter
    @rapid_diffs_presenter ||= ::RapidDiffs::MergeRequestPresenter.new(
      ::MergeRequests::VersionedMergeRequest.from_diff_options(@merge_request, rapid_diff_options),
      diff_view: diff_view,
      diff_options: rapid_diff_options,
      current_user: current_user,
      request_params: params,
      conflicts: conflicts_with_types
    )
  end

  def rapid_diff_options
    permitted = params.permit(:diff_id, :start_sha, :commit_id, :only_context_commits)

    {
      diff_id: permitted[:diff_id],
      start_sha: permitted[:start_sha],
      commit_id: permitted[:commit_id],
      only_context_commits: permitted[:only_context_commits]
    }.compact.merge(diff_options)
  end

  when /MergeabilityCheckIdentifierEnum/ then "identifies MergeRequest mergeability checks"
  when /eventable_types/ then "lists MergeRequest as an eventable type"
  when /noteable_types/ then "lists MergeRequest as a noteable type"

  when /Discussions|ResourceMilestoneEvents|Statistics/ then "routes API requests for MergeRequests"
  when /issue_keys/ then "extracts Jira issue keys from MergeRequest branches"
  when /referenced_merge_request_commit_shas/ then "resolves MergeRequest commit SHAs in references"
  when /MergeRequestReferenceFilter/ then "parses MergeRequest references in markup"
  when /records_for_nodes/ then "loads MergeRequest records for parsed references"
  when /enqueue_ref_workers_for_merge_requests/ then "enqueues reference workers for MergeRequests"
  when /in_operator_query_builder_params/ then "builds query params for MergeRequest event filters"
  when /object_type/ then "declares MergeRequest as a cycle analytics object type"
  when /mr_table/ then "selects MergeRequest table for cycle analytics"
  when /issuable_builder/ then "builds MergeRequest hook payload"
  when /merge_request\?/ then "tests whether a ref/object is a MergeRequest"
  when /create_merge_request_without_hooks/ then "imports a MergeRequest without hooks"
  when /remove_cached_external_diff/ then "clears MergeRequest external diff cache on export"
  when /copy_resource_events/ then "copies MergeRequest resource events during clone"
  when /namespace_id/ then "extracts MergeRequest namespace id"
  when /import_issues|create_comments/ then "imports legacy GitHub issues/comments as MergeRequests"
  when /IssueAndMergeRequestActions|MergeRequestActions|RelateActions/ then "defines quick actions for MergeRequests"
  when /record/ then "records MergeRequest assignment events"
  when /type/ then "types recent MergeRequest searches"
  when /build/ then "builds URLs for MergeRequests"
  when /system_usage_data/ then "counts MergeRequests in system usage data"
  when /AliasResolver/ then "resolves placeholder aliases for MergeRequest references"
  when /add_merge_request_shas/ then "keeps around MergeRequest SHAs via rake task"
  else "references MergeRequest"
  end

end
# group by area
rows.group_by { |r| r[0].split("/")[1] }.sort.each do |area, list|

  puts
end
